title: Next-Gen Academic Management: A Strategic Framework for Meta-Skills Integration in Sichuan Private HEls - Empowering 21st-Century Competency and Institutional Innovation
author: Che, Chi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Next-Gen Academic Management: A Strategic Framework for Meta-Skills Integration in Sichuan Private HEIs introduces an empirically grounded innovation designed to transform higher education management by embedding future-ready competencies into core institutional systems. Anchored in Stanford D. School’s Design Thinking methodology and developed through a multi-phase mixed-methods research strategy, this framework responds directly to the urgent need for scalable academic reform in China's rapidly evolving educational landscape. 
 This innovation framework emerged from the diagnostic analysis of ten private universities in Sichuan Province, where critical gaps were identified in curriculum rigidity, passive pedagogy, and outdated assessment structures. These findings, validated through PNImodified analysis and expert consultations, revealed that core meta-skills—Strategic Synthesis, Creative Agility, Relational Dynamics, and Adaptive Expertise—were significantly underdeveloped. In response, the framework introduces three integrated modules: • Agile Meta-Skills Integration (AMIM) – for curriculum redesign and flexible learning pathways, • Immersive Experiential Pedagogy (IEPM) – for learner-centered and real-world instructional engagement, and • Smart Meta-Skills Assessment (SMAM) – for AI-supported, competency-based evaluation systems. 
 More than a theoretical model, this innovation offers a step-by-step roadmap for institutional 
 implementation, supported by Kotter’s 8-Step Change Model to ensure system-wide adoption and sustainable impact. It is specifically designed to support private HEIs in under-resourced contexts, enabling them to align with national education reform goals while fostering graduates who are globally competent, ethically grounded, and professionally agile. 
 This innovation framework not only equips university leaders, faculty, and policy implementers with actionable strategies, but also contributes to the broader mission of promoting equity, innovation, and workforce readiness in higher education. It reflects a commitment to bridging the gap between educational policy and classroom reality—between institutional vision and student transformation. I would like to extend my deepest gratitude to the academic administrators, faculty teams, global education experts, and research participants who generously contributed their insights to this project. It is my sincere hope that this handbook serves as a meaningful tool for institutional advancement and a catalyst for future-facing educational reform in China and beyond.
